title: Mental contamination Beast Mode plug depth weirdness etc
display_title: Mental contamination Beast Mode plug depth weirdness etc
wikitext:
[]: Mental contamination, Beast Mode, plug depth weirdness etc.             Quote Post by Kodaemon » Sat Apr 02, 2011 1:57 pm Case 1: The Asuka Incident First, the Nerv crew note how the plug depth is over 100 and "Psychological contamination levels are in the red zone!". Next, we see the visualisation of the pilot's seat starting to dissolve, and Ritsuko warns that " the pilot will cease to be human " if they don't pull the plug back. Take a note of this. Here, they notice the blue goo and realize they're dealing with an Angel. Boom, then DMYSYS vs Bardiel ensues. Now, lets's jump to the aftermath: Asuka's laying on some sort of life-support machine (why does Nerv always have to make these look like coffins...), with the Angel-inhibiting pillars around her. Ritsuko says her line about possible mental contamination . Case 2: Beast Mode During the fight with Zeruel, Mari activates ZA BEASTO, talking about the Eva " losing its humanity ", whatever that might mean. (By the way, it's the second time Mari takes something another person said and turns it on its head: see Kaji's and her comments after Tunniel.) One of the various fun things Beast Mode involves is increasing the plug depth over a safe value. Again, the pilot's seat descends further into the plug, and again it dissolves in the visualisation. Ritsuko comments that " the pilot is willing to risk contamination ". Mari gets glowy eyes, stuff happens. Mari reverts to normal after all this. Case 3: GIVE Ayanami BACK After spraying a ridiculous fountain of blood from his mouth, Shinji's sitting in the unpowered Unit 01. Suddenly, he gets glowy eyes and WANTS Ayanami BACK. Unit 01 also gets glowy red eyes and other parts, an AT arm and a halo. Ritsuko asks herself whether " Unit 01 has gone beyond all human limitations " (this, along with the glowy eyes may suggest some sort of similarity to Beast Mode). Again the plug depth increases (though this time we don''t get to see the seat dissolving), Ritsuko urges Shinji to stop, or he'll lose his humanity . Inside the entry plug Shinji actually gets out of his seat and pushes on, somehow reaching through to Zeruel's core. Rei is there, and he pulls her out, resulting in Zeruel's tanging in the outside world, and the blood forming a Big Naked Rei. In Entry Plug World, Shinji and Rei embrace, which leads to BNR getting absorbed into the Eva. Unit 01 becomes a godlike energy being and a "new lifeform". Then Kaworu spears Unit 01 and it reverts to its normal physical shape and colours. At this point, we don't know what Shinji and Rei's physical status is inside the plug. Later, Unit 01 gets a similar quarantine treatment as Asuka with the pillars. *** Truth be told, I don't really know where I'm going with this, but hey, it's food for thought. One thing that's certain is that Asuka, Mari, Shinji and Rei have ALL been subjected to possible mental contamination.

[]:             Quote Post by DanAwful » Sat Apr 02, 2011 2:19 pm One thing I noticed when comparing Shinji reaching the limits of humanity with Mari's and Asuka's is that Shinji's event seems to be based purely on will power alone whereas the other examples are forced upon the Evangelion (beast mode with Mari and an Angel with Asuka). Something else I noticed: the movies seem to be carrying over another element from the TV series that I've never really seen mention elsewhere. If you notice every time Shinji gets into Eva-01 without his plug suit crazy shit happens. The first time it happens, in both the series and the films, Eva-01 reverts to beast mode; the second time Eva-01 acquires an S2 engine; and then the third time instrumentality is instigated. The films combined the second and third occurrences which has me wondering what will happen the next time Shinji goes casual. How can you top third impact?

[]:             Quote Post by Kodaemon » Sat Apr 02, 2011 2:28 pm Ha! Now that you mention it, he's not even wearing the headset, just like EoE!

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Apr 02, 2011 3:05 pm "losing their humanity" might mean "reverting to LCL", judging by the context its used in - these pillars seem to be an anti-angel measure. Bethany Base also has them.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sat Apr 02, 2011 3:09 pm For the record, it's almost definitely thecase that Rei and Shinji are in soul form inside the Eva, considering the dialogue of the preview of the next film.

[]:             Quote Post by DanAwful » Sat Apr 02, 2011 3:11 pm Another thing upon retrospection: the safety locks that come out of Eva-02s back look quite similar to the large spires at bethany base as well as the one's surrounding Asuka in the containment area.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Apr 02, 2011 3:14 pm AuraTwilight wrote: For the record, it's almost definitely thecase that Rei and Shinji are in soul form inside the Eva, considering the dialogue of the preview of the next film.

[]:             Quote Post by Kodaemon » Sat Apr 02, 2011 3:22 pm I take the pilot seat dissolving in the visualisations as a sign that it's entering some sort of other plane of existence, so yeah, Shinji probably wasn't corporeal at the time. Plus, there's the matter of Unit 01 itself apparently changing its form. However, I'm not quite sure what Shinji and Rei's state is after Kaworu spears Unit 01. Tanged a'la ep. 20 definitely seems plausible, but not 100% certain.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Apr 05, 2011 10:16 pm Regarding the "dissolving" plug interiors, the displays are probably misleading here. I'm pretty sure, in Asuka's case, it's a representation of the interior being engulfed by Bardiel's core (which creeps up through the plug from the bottom and entices the interior array toward Eva-03's core). In Mari's case, it's a representation of the entry plug interior becoming "hidden" and incapable of being monitored further. DanAwful wrote: Another thing upon retrospection: the safety locks that come out of Eva-02s back look quite similar to the large spires at bethany base as well as the one's surrounding Asuka in the containment area.

Next, we see the visualisation of the pilot's seat starting to dissolve, and Ritsuko warns that " the pilot will cease to be human " if they don&#3
